channel.title: AC Stepan WordWorks
channel.description: Recent content on AC Stepan WordWorks

item.title: About
item.description:
The Linguist

My academic training in Slavic linguistics gave me something that proved essential throughout a career in national security: an analytical framework for how language actually works — not just what it says, but how it encodes meaning, fails across cultures, and shapes thought. A PhD is a long way of learning to ask better questions, and those questions have followed me everywhere.

The Practitioner

Twenty-one years at the Department of Defense — most recently at the Defense Intelligence Senior Level — gave me a ground-level education in what linguistic failure actually costs. I worked at the intersection of language, intelligence, and AI/ML in environments where imprecision isn’t an abstract problem.

item.title: Contact
item.description:
Let’s Talk

Every engagement starts with a conversation. If you’re navigating a language governance challenge, deploying AI in a regulated or multilingual environment, or simply not sure whether you have a problem worth solving — that conversation is a good place to start.

Email: acstepanwordworks@gmail.com

LinkedIn: AC Stepan WordWorks

Anne Stepan is the Principal Consultant.

item.title: Resources
item.description:
Academic Work

My PhD dissertation in Slavic linguistics is available here for download. (Coming soon)

Writing

Essays and blog posts on language, AI governance, Czech heritage, and the places they intersect. (Coming soon)

Language Learning Resources

Curated resources for Czech and other Slavic languages — with notes on what actually works. (Coming soon)

AI & Language Tools

An annotated list of tools worth knowing, and their limitations. (Coming soon)

Texas Czech Legacy Project

I volunteer with the Texas Czech Legacy Project at UT Austin, which uses AI-assisted transcription to preserve recordings of a dying Czech dialect spoken by immigrant communities in rural Texas — continuing a family legacy of language preservation that traces back to my great-grandfather Jan Štěpán, who emigrated from Bohemia in 1889.

item.title: Services
item.description:
Language & AI Risk Assessment

A time-bound diagnostic that surfaces where language workflows, AI usage, and governance structures create risk — before that risk reaches the field or public domain. Delivers an executive-ready risk map with prioritized recommendations.

Engagement: 2–4 weeks · Fixed scope

Senior Language Advisory & AI

An ongoing advisory relationship providing expert judgment on high-stakes language decisions, AI usage boundaries, and regulatory alignment — without owning execution.

item.title: Welcome
item.description: This is where my writing will live — essays and posts on language, AI governance, Czech heritage, and the places they intersect.
